content.problemStatement:
You are given a binary string SSS of length NNN.

Define:


cnt00cnt_{00}cnt00‚Äã = the number of substrings `00` in SSS.
cnt11cnt_{11}cnt11‚Äã = the number of substrings `11` in SSS.
In one **flip**, you may choose any index iii and toggle SiS_iSi‚Äã (`0` becomes `1`, `1` becomes `0`).

Your task is to determine the **minimum number of flips** required to make cnt11‚â•cnt00cnt_{11} \ge cnt_{00}cnt11‚Äã‚â•cnt00‚Äã.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of **two lines**:

The first line contains an integer NNN ‚Äî the length of the binary string.
The second line contains the binary string SSS.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of flips required to make cnt11‚â•cnt00cnt_{11} \ge cnt_{00}cnt11‚Äã‚â•cnt00‚Äã.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first test case, we can flip the first zero achieving 10.

Now, cnt11=0cnt_{11} = 0cnt11‚Äã=0 is greater than or equal to cnt00=0cnt_{00} = 0cnt00‚Äã=0.



For the second test case, we make no flips achieving 01.

Now, cnt11=0cnt_{11} = 0cnt11‚Äã=0 is greater than or equal to cnt00=0cnt_{00} = 0cnt00‚Äã=0.



For the fifth test case, we flip the second zero achieving 011.

Now, cnt11=1cnt_{11} = 1cnt11‚Äã=1 is greater than or equal to cnt00=0cnt_{00} = 0cnt00‚Äã=0.
